topic: Sharp non-equivalence and strict-extension theorem between SDiD, augmented synthetic control / matrix completion, and staggered SDiD under bounded-rank interactive fixed effects.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp rank/span boundary at which original SDiD, ridge-ASCM, and nuclear-norm matrix completion induce the same ATT functional for every rank-R untreated panel.

source_refs.claim: SDiD combines unit weights and time weights and proves IFE consistency/asymptotic normality, but not a sharp finite-dimensional equivalence characterization against ASCM or matrix completion.
source_refs.author: Dmitry Arkhangelsky, Susan Athey, David A. Hirshberg, Guido W. Imbens, Stefan Wager

source_refs.claim: Matrix completion connects low-rank regularization, IFE, synthetic control, and unconfoundedness, but does not give necessary-and-sufficient equality conditions with SDiD or ASCM.
source_refs.author: Susan Athey, Mohsen Bayati, Nikolay Doudchenko, Guido W. Imbens, Khashayar Khosravi
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: ASCM debiases imperfect synthetic-control fit using an outcome model and ridge negative weights, but leaves the exact equality frontier with SDiD and MC unstated.
source_refs.author: Eli Ben-Michael, Avi Feller, Jesse Rothstein
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.excerpt: rank(Tau) <= K_star holds, where K_star is a sharp threshold
giving the input list and output algorithm for Gamma_m

open_problems.why_unsolved: The three literatures impose different population normalizations and optimization primitives: SDiD estimates two-sided unit/time weights, ASCM uses donor-side regression augmentation, and matrix completion recovers a global low-rank surface through nuclear-norm shrinkage. The prior ThmSmith rank-threshold attempt failed because the computable threshold object was not pinned to primitive matrices; the published papers likewise provide sufficient consistency conditions rather than a necessary-and-sufficient rank/span frontier.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient identification theorem giving an explicit observable kernel or rank certificate whose zero set is exactly the set of designs where the three estimators define the same ATT functional, plus a minimal counterexample family outside it.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a strict-extension lattice separating convex SCM, signed or ridge ASCM, SDiD with time weights, and nuclear-norm matrix completion under bounded-rank interactive fixed effects.

source_refs.claim: The synthesis paper relaxes SCM restrictions through intercepts, negative weights, non-unit-sum weights, and regularization, but does not prove a formal strict hierarchy among later SDiD, ASCM, and MC estimands.
source_refs.author: Nikolay Doudchenko, Guido W. Imbens

source_refs.claim: Penalized SCM shows different regularizations select different synthetic controls in disaggregated settings, exposing non-equivalent feasible-weight geometries.
source_refs.author: Alberto Abadie, Jeremy L'Hour
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Ridge ASCM can be written as a modified SCM problem allowing negative weights, directly raising extrapolation-versus-fit questions.
source_refs.author: Eli Ben-Michael, Avi Feller, Jesse Rothstein
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.excerpt: comparing convex weights ... against signed affine weights
row-span visibility is imposed as an assumption

open_problems.why_unsolved: Convex donor hulls, signed affine donor spans, two-sided SDiD contrasts, and low-rank completions are related but not obviously nested once regularization and time weighting enter. The prior proposal identified convex-versus-signed separation as reviewer-prescribed work but did not derive an exact boundary; the published papers establish individual estimators rather than inclusion and strict-separation witnesses for the whole family.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A lattice theorem of feasible identifying functionals, with every inclusion proved and every strict separation witnessed by a minimal rank-R panel with closed-form ATT limits.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize when staggered or cohort-time synthetic DiD collapses to a weighted sum of single-adoption SDiD or ASCM functionals, and when later-treated controls create a strictly different ATT functional.

source_refs.claim: Design-robust TWFE constructs assignment-model weights for general treatment paths including staggered adoption and links weighting functions to limiting estimands, but does not give a sharp equivalence theorem to SDiD or ASCM under IFE.
source_refs.author: Dmitry Arkhangelsky, Guido W. Imbens, Lihua Lei, Xiaoman Luo

source_refs.claim: Partially pooled SCM for staggered adoption balances both unit-specific and average treated-unit imbalance, showing that single-component balancing can bias the average effect.
source_refs.author: Eli Ben-Michael, Avi Feller, Jesse Rothstein
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B

source_refs.excerpt: not-yet-treated comparison cohort is anticipatory at t but not b
coefficient pooling before absolute values for an aggregate

open_problems.why_unsolved: Staggering changes both the missing-entry pattern and the target weighting: never-treated, not-yet-treated, and already-treated units can enter different cohort-time comparisons with different implicit counterfactual restrictions. The web literature gives estimator constructions, while prior reviewers isolated coefficient pooling and not-yet-treated contamination as unresolved formal cases.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient theorem on adoption support, cohort sizes, pre-period overlap, and rank R under which the staggered estimator equals a cohort-weighted sum of single-shock SDiD or ASCM functionals, with explicit strict-separation examples otherwise.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive an efficiency and asymptotic-variance ordering among SDiD, ASCM, matrix completion, and staggered synthetic-control estimators under one bounded-rank IFE nuisance model.

source_refs.claim: SDiD gives consistency and asymptotic normality under latent factors, but not a semiparametric efficiency comparison with ASCM or MC.
source_refs.author: Dmitry Arkhangelsky, Susan Athey, David A. Hirshberg, Guido W. Imbens, Stefan Wager

source_refs.claim: Factor-imputation matrix completion establishes normal distribution theory for ATT estimators under strong factor structure, on a different route from SDiD weighting.
source_refs.author: Jushan Bai, Serena Ng
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: For staggered DiD/event studies the paper derives robust and efficient estimators, but in an additive parallel-trends framework rather than the SDiD/ASCM/MC bounded-rank IFE geometry.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Xavier Jaravel, Jann Spiess

open_problems.why_unsolved: Each estimator's variance theory uses a different nuisance representation and uncertainty source: balancing-weight residuals, regression augmentation, low-rank factor estimation, assignment-design weights, or event-study imputation. A legitimate ordering requires one shared tangent space, one ATT target, and one rank-R nuisance class.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An influence-function representation and semiparametric efficiency bound for ATT under rank-R IFE, followed by variance dominance or non-dominance theorems for the limiting linear functional of each estimator.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the phase diagram for how ridge, SDiD quadratic, nuclear-norm, and penalized-SCM regularization sequences change the population ATT limit functional.

source_refs.claim: MC identifies through low nuclear-norm regularization and notes that causal-panel approaches differ in how they impose identification restrictions.
source_refs.author: Susan Athey, Mohsen Bayati, Nikolay Doudchenko, Guido W. Imbens, Khashayar Khosravi
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Ridge ASCM uses regularization to control pre-treatment fit while minimizing extrapolation from the convex hull.
source_refs.author: Eli Ben-Michael, Avi Feller, Jesse Rothstein
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Penalized SCM trades pairwise matching discrepancies against whole-synthetic-control matching discrepancies, making penalty choice part of the estimand geometry.
source_refs.author: Alberto Abadie, Jeremy L'Hour
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

open_problems.why_unsolved: Regularization is often justified through computation, prediction risk, or stability, but the identification question depends on whether penalties vanish, stay fixed, or scale with N and T. The papers do not share a penalty normalization, so equality of population functionals is not determined by existing asymptotics.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A pathwise limit theorem mapping penalty sequences to population ATT functionals, with explicit phase transitions where SDiD, ASCM, MC, or penalized SCM become equal or separate.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove a sharp support-function interval for synthetic-control treatment effects under bounded deterministic factor misspecification and identify the scalar diagnostics that are generically nonsharp.

source_refs.excerpt: sharp partial-identification theorem for the post-period treatment effect
State Conjecture 1 with an explicit constant

open_problems.why_unsolved: The banked proposal had a plausible finite-dimensional projection argument and a two-dimensional witness, but reviewers rejected the positioning because scalar reporting and minimal sharp statistics were not nailed down. The remaining obstacle is a generic open-set theorem showing exactly which reported pre-fit summaries cannot determine the sharp endpoint.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A support-function sharpness theorem for the Chebyshev-tube identified set, together with an open-neighborhood nonsharpness theorem for fixed scalar RMSPE/MSPE diagnostics and an explicit tangent-LP endpoint certificate.

literature_map:
Abadie and Gardeazabal (2003) and Abadie, Diamond, and Hainmueller (2010, 2015) supply the convex-weight SCM foundation; Abadie (2021 JEL) codifies feasibility and data-requirement guidance.
Doudchenko and Imbens (2016) synthesize SCM, regression, and DiD as restrictions on intercepts, sum constraints, nonnegativity, and regularization.
Xu (2017) generalizes SCM to interactive fixed effects, making latent-factor rank central for later SDiD and MC work.
Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, and Khosravi (2021 JASA) cast causal panel estimation as nuclear-norm matrix completion and connect MC, IFE, SCM, and unconfoundedness.
Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens, and Wager (2021 AER) introduce SDiD: unit weights plus time weights, with IFE consistency and asymptotic normality.
Ben-Michael, Feller, and Rothstein (2021 JASA) introduce ASCM/ridge ASCM as bias correction for imperfect SCM fit with controlled negative-weight extrapolation.
Bai and Ng (2021 JASA) give factor-imputation normal theory for ATT under strong factor structures, another MC-adjacent route.
Ferman and Pinto (2021 QE) show imperfect pre-treatment fit can bias SCM and that demeaned variants can improve bias/variance relative to DiD.
Abadie and L'Hour (2021 JASA) address multiplicity/interpolation bias for disaggregated synthetic controls through penalization.
Ben-Michael, Feller, and Rothstein (2022 JRSS-B) extend SCM to staggered adoption via partial pooling of unit-specific and average treated imbalance.
Goodman-Bacon (2021), Sun and Abraham (2021), Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021), and Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024) expose weighting/heterogeneity and efficiency issues in staggered DiD, mostly outside low-rank SDiD/MC geometry.
Arkhangelsky and Imbens (2022 Econometrics Journal) and Arkhangelsky, Imbens, Lei, and Luo (2024 QE) push panel identification toward assignment/design weights and double robustness, creating tension with pure low-rank recovery.
Cattaneo, Feng, and Titiunik (2021 JASA) and related SC inference papers treat uncertainty as prediction/sensitivity, underscoring that variance comparisons remain method-specific.
The live frontier is a sharp equivalence/separation calculus: rank R, pre-period count, donor count, adoption support, and penalty scaling should determine when SDiD, ASCM, MC, and staggered synthetic-weight estimators target the same ATT functional.
prior_proposal_map:
Inspected current qid directory panel_sdid_mc_id/: only state and pipeline JSONL existed; pipeline had init-only signal and no prior proposal content.
Inspected _bank/failed/eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1 README, final/rejected proposals, and reviewer JSON; high signal for estimator non-equivalence, active pre-period EIF rank, quotient IF comparison, and spectral Gamma/K_star thresholds.
Inspected _bank/downgraded/pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1 README, proposal, and reviewer JSON; high signal for synthetic-control factor-misspecification partial ID, scalar RMSPE nonsharpness, signed versus convex weights, and support-function calibration.
Inspected _bank/candidates/pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 README, proposal, and reviewer JSON; useful signal for staggered-adoption anticipation boxes, signed coefficient pooling, and not-yet-treated comparison contamination.
Inspected _bank/failed/panel_honest_did_phase_v1 README, final/rejected proposals, and reviewer JSON; adjacent panel signal for aggregation-rank phase diagrams, rank-loss discontinuity, and active-face sensitivity paths.
Scanned relevant legacy/bank path names including q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends, q1_minimal_basis_p1_staggered, q1_spectral_threshold/phase_transition, and eid_lp_svar_nonequiv; retained only stronger panel/synthetic-control/staggered-DiD proposal signals above.
Pipeline JSONL was used sparingly: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1_pipeline.jsonl contained only creation metadata and yielded no candidate open problem.
